dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have these purifying salts here, sir. You're welcome to make use of them, especially if you help form a perimeter...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Very well.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Keep your distance from me, witch. My club is readily at hand. The Satyrs and the Nymphs likewise were warned.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh I don't plan on moving from this spot, except to leave reluctantly. I shouldn't be doing this, but I'm cold and tired.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Cold and tired... now we're speaking the same language. How did you learn of such a place? Even the other gods don't know of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We have a hot spring much like this where I am from. Well... the atmosphere is very different, and there are far fewer in attendance typically.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Not what I meant. Why are you here? You don't strike me as the... drinking-feasting sort.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're one to talk. Although you did mention having a drinking contest with Lord Dionysus at some point. I don't know why he lets me in. But I like passing through.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Hm. Bathing with Satyrs for a change, instead of slaughtering them. Anything to break up the routine....

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well...! I feel refreshed and yet uncomfortable. Bye, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was going to take a quick dip once this pool is sufficiently purified if you'd like to as well, sir.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Aye.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Dionysus understands something the other gods do not. Do you know what it is, sister?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You don't mean something simple like debauchery, do you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: He understands perfection cannot be attained. The rest keep striving for it, demanding it from one another, claiming to have it themselves. All a farce.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're one of the greatest warriors that ever lived. I don't believe you haven't pursued perfection. I've pursued it... I never feel good enough but that hasn't stopped me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I never said I've not pursued it. My claim is, the pursuit is meaningless... heading for a destination that can never be reached. All because others expect it of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You also don't seem the sort to be overly concerned with what others think of you. Except perhaps this mountain's Queen and King.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Why am I even speaking to you? Only Atlas himself knows what it's like... bearing the weight of the world.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well this was pleasant for the most part. Take care, sir.

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: Seems like there's room enough...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: I'll just purify these waters first...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: Carry on, everyone, don't mind me...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: Just a brief soak...

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: ...What do you think you're looking at?

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: ...I am attempting to recover here...

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: ...Do I appear as though I'm in the mood for chat?

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: ...These aches of mine are stubborn, but they'll burn off...

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: ...Don't get any closer to me, witch.

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: ...At least the blasted water's finally purified again.

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...The waters, nothing more...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Likewise. Still a bit of a climb left...

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Not in a talking mood either.

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...They always do.

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...Oh I'm staying right here.

options.speaker: PlayerUnit
options.text: ...These Salts work wonders.

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: You never saw me, I never saw you. Scoot.

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: You think you know me, sister. But you don't.

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: Whatever you're about to say to me, don't.

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: You never saw me.

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: Been a pleasure.

options.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
options.text: Till next we meet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You. Things are about to turn ugly, and I require neither assistance nor witnesses, sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm passing through. And I'm not leaving here until the traitors to the House of Hades lie broken at our feet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Fine, then. We'll each earn our share. Though the lion's share is coming with me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: There's no more work here to be done... but it never ends. No hard feelings, by the way, about before.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Oh, none at all. I always wondered what it'd feel like to get struck full-on by the force of your club, and now I don't.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Nor do I like leaving any room for doubt. And if you need a reminder about my club, you know where to locate me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You're not what I thought. Kept your mouth shut about me. Even somehow overcame Prometheus after I butted in. Why and how?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I have my reasons and my ways. Why should I be forthcoming about them? You've not exactly been eager to open up.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Thought if I asked nicely, but that doesn't work on me either. Well, suit yourself. Not like I can drag it out of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You fight both up close and from afar. Seems inefficient. Choose one or the other and master it. Don't get fancy.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: One can excel at each. The goddess Artemis may favor her bow, but she's as ruthless as you up close, believe me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Once captured one of her woodland pets and learned that the hard way, so you don't have to persuade me. Fine. Think you're as good as she is, suit yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Why does a witch go learning to hoist a weapon such as that? You have your tricksy spells, yet bloody your hands.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I needed to gain endurance as well as avoid becoming overly dependent on magick, which can be physically exhausting.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Magick can be physically exhausting. You're an odd one, sister. But you've an eye for weaponry at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Your blood runs red. You're not entirely a goddess. We truly get the best of both worlds.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You were born to a mortal mother, weren't you? My mother's half-mortal. Death itself is in our blood. But you're different...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Different. Neither god, nor mortal, nor Shade. It's as you say; death itself is in my blood. All mortals are born to die. I was born also to kill.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Friendly word of advice: beware a large and not-entirely-dead boar snuffing about in the Ephyra alleyways. It's come for me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're being hunted by a boar, not the other way around? Did I hear that correctly?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: We... had our troubles in the past while it was more... alive. This is what I get for not finishing the job when I had the chance.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: How is that dog of yours if he resides still underneath the earth? I grew somewhat partial to him as beasts go.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mean Cerberus? I never knew him growing up but see him rather often recently. He's been active, and doing very well. I can't believe he didn't snap your head off back then...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, he tried. And using all three maws. And he was heavy. When next you ascend Olympus, thank the Fates you're not encumbered with the Hound of Hell.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You seem to know the Boatman pretty well. Worked together before?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I've had occasion to visit your realm in the past. The Boatman is my supplier. And evidently yours as well... buying up all the merchandise.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I can't believe I'm asking, but would you be interested in attending an exclusive feast with Lord Dionysus and his close associates? There's plenty to drink, a sizable bath...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: What do you mean, there's a sizeable bath? All he ever talked about was the drink. Where shall it be, and when?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Where is difficult for me to explain but in one of the mountain's interior chambers. And as for when, I think it's ongoing without an end in sight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Well you make a compelling offer, witch. A feast that's dragged on for gods-know-how-long and you don't know how to get there. I'll give it some serious consideration.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You live. By my good graces, granted, but... first I've seen somebody stand against the filth that's overtaken this accursed town.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I aim to cleanse the filth down to the source. He resides deep in the Underworld, though I've business to attend to on Olympus first.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: What a coincidence! I just returned from there not long ago. So the gods are putting all the pieces into place.... You watch yourself, sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Titan Prometheus seems rather pleased of late. Why do you suppose that is?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: How should I know, and why should it matter? Seeing as we don't discuss him here, or anywhere.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Then let's discuss you. Have you gotten what you sought? With the Fates declaring that an age of mortals is to come.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm a mere half-god, as you may recall. I never get what I want. But when finally comes a time for change, I'll welcome it. May that next age no longer need for gods or men like me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Having given it all some further thought, I must admit I undersold what you achieved, sister. Your task was difficult.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It could have been worse. I had the strength of mighty Heracles on my side, at least for the most part. But thank you; that means a lot coming from you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: No need. For now, it seems we get to ply our trade without the pressure to accomplish the impossible. I'll take it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're not the only one who had high expectations to live up to, Heracles. I'm Princess of the Underworld. My father and mother were taken by Chronos, and I was raised to slay him. Repeatedly told it was imperative, but near impossible.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Didn't ask for your life story, but sure. The Fates had big plans for you. It'd be a lot of pressure were the outcome not already preordained.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And what of your various victories? You've had to fight for your every achievement, and bitterly. None of those were preordained?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: We don't know what is preordained or not, and can ill-afford taking any chances. But whichever burdens you've been carrying do nothing to alleviate mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your struggles are unchanged knowing you're not alone?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Everywhere I go, the dead know me; it's irksome. Used to be able to show up to a slaughter without drawing attention.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The consequence of notoriety. The Shades where I'm from all think the world of you! We may or may not even have a wall-scroll depicting your adventures.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: They don't know anything about me, only the falsehoods and half-truths. I don't need a cult of mindless followers.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: If you're a daughter of Hades for true, that makes us cousins, doesn't it? Although knowing my father, you must have a lot of those...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've met quite a few recently among the Olympians themselves. But I grew up mostly in solitude.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Thank the Fates for that. Fewer relatives means fewer obligations... fewer wars to be recruited into through no doing of your own, other than being born...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Word is the Fates themselves are gone. Too bad. I always wanted that job for myself. Not that they can be killed.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You always wanted to kill the Three Fates... for deciding you'd become a great hero of exceeding strength whose achievements would inspire countless others?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Certainly if not for them I wouldn't be here. Well now they know what it's like... getting strung about with no say in the matter. The Fates be damned! Justice is all we need.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Our fathers sure made a mess of things for us, didn't they, sister. Born to the mightiest of gods, yet living like outcasts... like bloody animals.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's Chronos that made a mess of things. If he'd have just stayed dead, we'd be at peace. And together with our fathers, no doubt.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Have you met our fathers? Sure, we're in the midst of an interminable war, but at least we're far away from them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...You have her blessing, then. Just like that, she gave you her strength. Her approval...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You mean Hera? Of course she did; we're all on the same side. More than that, we're family.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Family. It figures that her grace the Queen presides over the entire concept. Marriage, birthing, and the like. Curses, in a charitable form.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: In all your travels back and forth from Mount Olympus, have you ever seen a young man with wings soaring over the waters, dropping explosives to disrupt the Titan's fleets?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: The son of the artificer Daedalus. Those are not real wings, and he's a Shade now. He and his father both. What of him?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, it's just that... he's quite an admirer of yours, apparently. He'd give anything to meet you at some point. I thought perhaps I could facilitate an introduction.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Not interested in admirers. Especially ones whose drowned corpses I had to bury. He's better off with the idea of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Icarus, the son of Daedalus we spoke of, he was very moved to learn you may have helped administer his final rites. He said to tell you cheers... I think he'd want to say much more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Urgh. Would you just leave me be with all his sentiment.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Well, if it isn't my old Lion Fang. I had multiples to spare, and you looked like you could use some of its savagery yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Its strength is useful certainly, though why give it away? The stronger I am, the more I can encroach on your glory.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Simple, sister: I don't want it anymore. Neither the Fang nor the glory. Take my work for all I care; there's always more.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: That Lion Fang still giving you results? Great strength that fades with time... like the experience of being mortal.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To feel one's body beginning to give way to decrepitude... fortunately, your Fang recovers all its potency each night.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Unlike the lion from which it came. They said it sprang from the moon, can you believe that rubbish? But it was a noble beast...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Those magick tricks... you're borrowing that power. How can you trust it when strength belongs to you alone?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Magick and raw strength are not in opposition; both ultimately come from within, they're just trained differently.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I don't think so, sister. You're drawing from the moon, and the earth, and... who knows what else. But in this world, you can't rely on anybody but yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You ever see Lady Medea around here? Long dark hair and attire. She could probably help thin the crowd. No denying we're outnumbered.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Already owe her for a time I wasn't... at my best. Won't be asking further favors from a witch. You take more than you give.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What did witches ever do to you? It sounds as though she helped you out, yet you still speak of her with such disdain.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I know what you're capable of. I respect the power of an erupting volcano, doesn't mean I want to be near one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: That stone club of yours... that is a worthy killing instrument. I'd wrest it from your grip, but it's probably bound to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're wise to restrain yourself, as the Rock Lion Mace answers to me. Not quite as supple as your own club... olive-wood, is it?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Aren't you the wellspring of experience? Knowing your clubs as well as your trees. I like mine better than yours anyway.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Olympus is fortunate to have your strength and service, Son of Zeus.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I serve the gods all right. Served them a long time, one tall order after another. They've brought me fame, glory... everything. They never get enough of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But you've had enough of them. Then... what are you doing here?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, my work history can demonstrate I never get enough of them, either. I take a job, I do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to your ancestral mountain, sister. Everything you dreamed of? I once yearned to be here, too. I got over it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Yet you're still defending this place. You speak as though you've lost your purpose, but I've seen you fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Purpose. The gods asked the world of me before I could join them here, only to have to defend their honor some more. You'll find your own purpose, soon enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You're known as perhaps the perfect warrior, Heracles, yet don't seem too caught up in that pursuit. You fight savagely, with brute force rather than finesse. You want results.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I do. So what. Perfection is a result. And why don't you stay out of my psyche, witch? I don't need you in my head any more than in my presence.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I just want to understand the things you've said... because I don't want to end up like you, filled with regret despite my excellence. Your greatness is lost only on you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You don't have to worry, sister. You'll never end up like me. Because you'll never be like me. No one ever has, or shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You made it out before. Cyclops must have caught your scent, though. He's strong, that one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That's a ringing endorsement. Though you must be stronger still! Ever considered putting him to the test? Could save me a lot of trouble.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: He's not my type. Besides, he's a spawn of the gods, just like you and me. Wouldn't be proper if we were all fighting all the time, now would it?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Have you had any run-ins with the Titan Prometheus while on Olympus recently? He seems to be the one leading the assault forces besieging your family's home.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Is he now? That's rich. Wasn't he supposed to be tied up on some mountain someplace else, getting picked apart?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How do you not know he's free? You should be hunting him, not this fodder. Who's setting your objectives?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: My orders come directly from the King and Queen. If you've any concerns with how I'm carrying out my work, just go complain to them.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Last we had a skirmish such as this, after the dust settled, you were nowhere to be found. Fled, like a thief in the night.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I had to make an early exit. Figured you would have no trouble resolving the matter, seeing as you work alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: That I do. But I don't like combatants disappearing on me. Stay where I can see you, or stay out of the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You talk too much, you know that, witch? Dispense with the pleasantries, and we could be killing even more.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm not certain which pleasantries you mean, but if you'd like to speak even less, that's certainly your prerogative.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Speaking never was my specialty. And as you may have surmised, I'm more the sort to focus on my strengths.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Where did you get that scythe of yours, sister? You don't seem like much of a farmer, though neither did Thanatos.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Would you believe me if I told you that his mother was letting me borrow it for a little while?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sir, what are you doing in the middle of the Rift of Thessaly. How did you even get here? Although, you must be a powerful swimmer.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm proficient in a wide variety of athletics, since you mention it. Swimming, leaping, striking things with clubs.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So this is part of your patrol path? To think my journey could have been even more infested with foes if not for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Much would be different if not for me. Though you're one of the few I've met of late to admit it. Fair voyaging, sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I'd given you no thought at all while you were gone. Otherwise I would have figured you had given up.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I don't know what I'm supposed to say to that except I hadn't given you much thought lately either. Is there anyone you do regularly think about?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I think mainly of the bodies I have crushed beneath my club and feet. You're welcome to join them, at any point.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: No thank you, that's all right.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: They said the war was finally drawing to a close. Somebody must have forgotten to notify those we slaughtered.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: We need them here for the time being anyhow. Has Olympus informed you of the nature of the next phase of our plan?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I got the gist: We fight on. Nothing changes. Never has before, so... why would it now?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What are you talking about? A lot's changed! Olympus is no longer in danger. The House of Hades is restored.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What will you do when all of this is over, sir? Or rather, what would you desire to do?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Why ask? I tend not to give desire too much thought, beyond any immediate and bestial cravings I may have.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I would appreciate any perspective, that's all. All my life I had an obligation to my family to fulfill. I'm not certain what to do with myself now that I have...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I am the last individual whose perspective you should heed, sister. Chase your dreams if you still have any. Now scoot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You make it to the mountaintop as yet? In case you haven't, nothing good awaits you there. Or so I hear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What, you mean Typhon? I'm surprised the Olympians don't have you helping out with him, instead of bashing a bunch of weaklings far from where the real danger is.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: The Olympians won't abide some half-god stealing their glory. I get the drudgery, not the important work. Thank the Fates that you get some of each.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: What is it like... being up against that monster at the summit. I've not faced something of that size. Not yet at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've not faced anything of Typhon's size either. His enormity is to his disadvantage in some ways, but facing him, a torrential storm, and his broodlings all at once can be a lot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: To think the multi-headed Hydra I once slew was merely one of his offspring. But there shall be worse monsters to fight in ages hence...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You must be rather comfortable up here. This land has become near as dismal as the one from which you hail.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: How would you even know what all the Underworld is like? It couldn't be more different. Stop by sometime and see.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Already had my fill. Ask your father sometime about when last I visited. If you ever see him or his dog ever again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You really have been to the Underworld before... and dragged my family's dog all the way to the surface, with my father's consent?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Your father didn't think I had it in me. Another of the many gods who failed to take me at my word. But fear not, I let the dog return. He's fortunate he's not a cat.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Unbelievable. Then why didn't Olympus send you in after Chronos if you have that sort of experience?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: They've been on the defensive. They probably don't think I could do it. And they may not trust me. Who knows why else.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You've more aptitude for weaponry than one might expect. I once sought to master every armament under the sun, but in the end... I settled for the simple club.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I am bearer of the Arms of Night in all their various Aspects; I'd never settle for just one. Do you not fear you've grown complacent?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: No. Tread this path long enough, you'll find that any weapon can do the job. At that point, there's no real choice. Though I still carry all these other arms just to put on a show.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I know what you are. You bloody witches. One foreign word... one finger-wagging gesture directed at me... and I'll squeeze you till you pop.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That won't be necessary... though I would know how came you to feel this way about us bloody witches? How many have you met?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Two questions too many. The more you know, the more you can manipulate. Now, off with you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You're the help? Oh, that's rich. Friendly word of advice, sister: Stay out of my bloody way, else you're liable to get hurt. Now scoot, and tell whomever sent you that I work alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your threat is duly noted, Son of Zeus. I'm headed for the docks, but understand this city's sealed shut.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Then you understand you ought turn back. This town is dead, and doesn't let the living pass.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm Daughter of Hades; the dead shall answer to me. They may just need a little encouragement.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Try all you like. But remember you were warned.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Great Son of Zeus, I offer you this gift of Nectar, that it may quench your thirst as you labor for the gods. They must be grateful for your might.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Heh. Saves me having to find another bottle for when next I have to prove my fealty. Here, something for your trouble.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure you don't want to give me only half the bottle there? Seeing as I'm merely half a god... I got their strength but seldom get their luxury.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nothing's gained from luxury in excess, anyway. But there are times a little doesn't hurt! May this soothe your spirit when the occasion arises.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You truly take me for a fool, believing I would drink from this? You could have ensorcelled the contents in any number of ways, to drive me to madness, or worse...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I swear to you upon my father's name that I've done no such thing. And it would have obviously spoiled if I had. Do you want to see me drink it first?

endLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
endLines.text: No. I can dispose of it myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: My favor, in exchange for drink? No. You waste your resources and time; my time as well. You'll get a share of spoils if you earn your kills, but nothing more.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I understand. Nevertheless, we're both working for the same family, and I have seen firsthand the efforts you're taking to drive back their enemies. And I'm grateful.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: They never give this stuff to me themselves, the mountain gods. Developed a distaste for it as a result, especially after a certain... contest with the god of wine. But that was long ago.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You tried to out-drink Lord Dionysus? Even with all your fortitude, that sounds like a very dangerous undertaking from what I understand.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You say the gods never shared Nectar with you; well, I'm sharing some now. Perhaps you can regain a taste for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: No more of this, witch. I told you I don't care for it, and presently I have no plans to go make any offerings myself. My tolerance for you likewise nears its limit.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Your tolerance is enough, sir. I see how hard you work, expecting nothing in return. For all you've given to Olympus, may you never give your soul.

endLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
endLines.text: ...That would require me to have one still.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You said I wasn't what you thought; neither are you what I expected, sir. I'd heard tales of your exploits, and truly never seen so fierce a fighter. But I know there's more to you. Here, then! For one of your divinity, a divine gift.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Ambrosia... you would take me for another god. I am half-man; such stuff is not for me, and you sully its purpose merely in the offering. What do you want from me?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I want you to accept that there are gods and goddesses who hold you in esteem. If falling short of perfection means falling short of godhood, then none of us are divine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: ...Now you're on to something, sister. Well, if and when the rest of the family gets around to accepting your terms, let's all share this bottle. I'll just hang onto it for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh, it's you. Either get in or get out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Fear not, sister. We are at rest here rather than at work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: No need for killing at the moment here, sister...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: How long since these waters were last purified...?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Look wine-god, you've another guest...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Touch me again, Satyr, and by the gods I'll... urgh...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: If you're starved for conversation, you'll have better fortune with the Boatman there.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Save it for somebody who cares, sister.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I look like I want to have ourselves a chat?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Urgh. You just made me lose count of all my blasted coin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: The Boatman cannot be negotiated with at all, it seems.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Not even a hello for his returning customers from that one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm working here, sister. Care to pitch in, fine, but don't get underfoot.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Much as I'd love to have ourselves a chat, I'm on the job. And better get to work.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You look like you've a taste for blood. Come get your fill for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Well don't just stand there, sister; we've a war here to perpetuate.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Enjoy the silence, sister. We'll have company here any moment now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Need a break from all the slaughter, just stand back. Far back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: The gods must want for us to give another glorious performance, huh.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Why am I even talking to you, sister? You save your breath, I'll save mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: We get to know each other, you'll live to regret it. Happens all the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Suggest remaining far afield of me at all times, witch.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You blasted gods and goddesses are all alike...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Go about your business, sister. I'm on the job.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: This vessel's going back to the bottom of the sea, whether you're here or not.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Rough waters ahead, sister. End up overboard, I won't be diving in to get you out.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Another stowaway aboard. And here I thought I was the only one.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: The sea took these ships' crews once before, and it's about to take them back.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: This ship's a mess, but let me go ahead and swab the deck, if you'll stand clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: What a coincidence, I was just heading to the mountain of the gods myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I've an urge to smash this ship to splinters, but then we'd be forced to swim.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I claim this ship in the name of the gods, although you're one of them, aren't you?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Not much room here to maneuver, so best stay far afield of my club.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: The darkness of these waters brings about a certain peace, doesn't it, sister?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Go someplace else. This blasted mountain's defenses are already spread too thin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: The gods require all the aid they can muster, which is why we're both here.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Oh good. I was beginning to feel lonesome having to shoulder all the work myself...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Suggest you stand clear before you're driven back from this mountain like all the rest.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Everybody wants a piece of Olympus lately, don't they. But it's not for the taking.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Best watch your step in all this ice and snow. Wouldn't want you to slip.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Now what could be more glorious than crushing Satyrs into frozen earth?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I was here first, sister. Go find your own Harpies and such to massacre.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: This is just a warm-up for what's to come later this evening, isn't it, sister?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I'll pull my strikes should you happen to get in the way here, but at the summit? No.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Soon you shall face me and the Flame Thief once again, but let's keep up the act for now.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I'm just about to start killing everything that moves, other than you and your pet.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Credit to the most efficient killer here; you do the work, you get the coin.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: It's been too long since last we slaughtered everything that moved about this place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: While you've been navigating your father's catacombs, I've had all the glory to myself.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: We're making a regular habit of crushing the life from anything that moves.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You're bloodied, so you'd best stand back and leave the taking of the glory all to me.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Hold, sister. Remember, not a word about before, or else. We can have ourselves another happy massacre and then be on our way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: And then perhaps we'll meet again on my way to the summit, if the conditions are just right. Wouldn't that be nice?

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Sure it would. We understand each other, then. Now to the bloodletting at hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Mistook you for the goddess of the hunt, stalking about these streets. Let's see if you can pick your targets off as well as she.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Isn't that axe a little big for you, sister? Though I laud the effort. We'll soon see if you can put it to the proper use.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Not often I'm envious of another's weaponry, but that is a thing of beauty you have there. Show me what it can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: You wield a scythe much like the one carried by Death himself. Here, we shall have to take his place.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: Look who decided to show her shining face again. You want to earn some extra coin, have I the opportunity for you.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Heracles_01
dialogueLines.text: I heard the tidings of your victory, but knew this wasn't over. Always more to be done. Now, come on.
